title: Gastrointestinal tract histology
body_html:
The table below describes the functions of the 4 layers of the gastrointestinal (GI) tract:

LayerNotes
MucosaCan be further subdivided into:
  • epithelium
  • lamina propria
  • muscularis mucosae
SubmucosaContains Meissner's (submucosal) plexus - primarily secretion and blood flow
Muscularis externaContains Auerbach's (myenteric) plexus - primarily controls motility of GI smooth muscle
Serosa / adventitiaIntraperitoneal = serosa
Retroperitoneal = adventitia

Diagram showing the main layers of the gastrointestinal tract
